author: @reypolice5231
text:
Just say

Though things are notorious for tipping over with senior citizens on them.

I could just imagine One loaded up, and the wheel being turned to sharply in the whole thing flips.

Maybe not since there's been a lot of weight on the back, don't know.

Could it be turned into a quad front end?

I saw one today with rocker arms for independent movement of the front in turns.

author: @scotttovey
text:
Hmm. I think I would have first added a cage that kept the cart from touching my back and then added an extension to the base that the cart sits on.

Might be more work on my part but.. I would want the buggy whole.
I think I have BBS - Bigger is Better Syndrome.
Could just be an American boomer thing too. 😏

author: @docink6175
text: respectfully need to disagree, there are numerous stores here that actually pay people to go out and recover their buggies/trollies/carts, some will just pay a small reward and a few really dont care. How ever its illegal for anyone to scrap a buggy or even parts of one w/o a proper letter on business head paper, scrap yards will not take them.. On the flip side Ive never heard of anyone getting a ticket or being arrested over a shopping buggy... I do like the basket idea

author: @krzysztofniedoba1905
text: Hello, the basket coul be a Faraday cage :)  if you combine several of them you could protect the trike against the storm :))

replies.author: @totherarf
replies.text: You might need several of them one inside the other ...... all dependent on the frequency of the magnetic field you want to protect from. If you are worried about your standard mains hum (50hz) you will need the holes in your cage to be less than 2 cm! ;o)

replies.author: @krzysztofniedoba1905
replies.text: @totherarf Ok! what about the microwaves? how big holes do I need?  :) And most important: how one does the trike totally invisible to radars (stealth)  :))

replies.author: @totherarf
replies.text:
@krzysztofniedoba1905 20 cm ... dependent on which microwave frequency you are using! Not that you would get me in one for protection with those gaps. The industry has set their limits at around 1 mm for safety .... a good healthy safety factor! Just have a peek in your kitchen to see how small the holes in the door mesh are ;o)

Do remember that different wavelengths can "bend" to different degrees though!

If you want radar invisibility you can't have it! You can only reduce the bounce a radar would see! Making it out of paper would be good then the only real bounce would be off the bits of metal like the chain and wheel rims! You could go down the stealth plane route you would also make the construction with many different angles and planes (sic). 

There is an "Invisibility" thing being researched for tanks in the UK .... but I would have to kill you if you found out ..... and myself if you told me ..... but it is based on a magnetic field produced by the tank and is reported to go down to the visible spectrum, but I know nothing, that is my story ..... and I'm sticking to it!

replies.author: @Arachnoid_of_the_underverse
replies.text: @totherarf  So if I magnetised my motorbike I could avoid " safety cameras" lol
